name: The Bank Of Bennington
description_short: Full-service mutual community bank headquartered in Bennington, VT with branches across Vermont. FDIC-insured with 108 years of operations and a 5-star Bauer rating.
description_long:
The Bank of Bennington is a mutual community bank established in 1979 (based on customer testimonial) and currently operating for 108 years of combined history. Headquartered in Bennington, Vermont, the bank operates as a full-service financial institution with branches in Bennington, Arlington, Manchester, and Rutland. As a mutual bank, it is owned by depositors rather than external shareholders, allowing profits to be reinvested directly into the communities it serves. The bank emphasizes its local decision-making authority and commitment to keeping money within Vermont communities.

The Bank of Bennington offers a comprehensive range of banking products including checking accounts with multiple tiers, savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (currently offering 3.75% APY on 5-year CDs with $500 minimum), online banking, mortgage applications, and home equity applications. Their checking account offerings are differentiated by lifestyle needs, from no-frills everyday accounts to interest-bearing accounts with additional benefits, plus student-friendly options. The bank also provides loan servicing through a dedicated team and maintains online banking capabilities for account management and applications.

The bank distinguishes itself through several competitive factors: it maintains a 5-star Bauer rating for 35 years, employs a mutual ownership structure that prioritizes community reinvestment, and emphasizes personalized local customer service with staff who "know customers by name." It has been recognized as one of the Best Places to Work in Vermont for 2024. The bank actively markets its commitment to small business support and community involvement, backed by customer testimonials from local business owners. It also provides security resources covering identity theft awareness, fraud prevention, and privacy rights education.

The primary caveat is that as a regional Vermont bank, The Bank of Bennington has limited geographic reach (4 branch locations in one state) and may not be accessible to out-of-state consumers without relying solely on online banking. CD rates of 3.75% APY are competitive but not exceptionally high compared to online-only banks. The website content is limited and does not provide comprehensive detail on loan products, interest rates for other products, or fee structures. Minimum deposit requirements for CD products ($500) may deter some savers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with multiple tier options
Certificates of Deposit (5-year CDs at 3.75% APY)
Privacy rights information and compliance resources
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
5-star Bauer rating maintained for 35 consecutive years indicates financial stability
Mutual ownership structure means profits reinvested into communities rather than distributed to shareholders
Local decision-making authority with staff trained to know customers by name
Competitive CD rates at 3.75% APY with flexible $500 minimum deposit
Named Best Places to Work in Vermont 2024, suggesting strong employee satisfaction and service quality
Dedicated loan servicing team with specific phone line (802-442-1640) for customer support
Online banking and digital mortgage/home equity applications available for convenience
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to 4 branches in Vermont only, creating accessibility challenges for out-of-state customers
Website provides minimal information on loan products, mortgage rates, or fee schedules for checking/savings accounts
No information available about overdraft policies, minimum balance requirements for checking accounts, or other account restrictions
Lacks details on business loan products, SBA lending, or commercial banking services despite marketing to business owners
best_for: Vermont residents seeking personalized local banking with community reinvestment values
Small business owners in Vermont looking for relationship-based lending and local decision-making
Conservative savers seeking FDIC-insured products from a financially stable mutual bank
Customers prioritizing local community impact over large national bank convenience
diagnosis: The Bank of Bennington is best for Vermont residents and small business owners seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with genuine community reinvestment and local decision-making. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Vermont with only 4 branch locations, making it impractical for consumers outside the state without exclusive reliance on online banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
